mapping.a_element: evaluating a multiverse by the single worst universe it contains (worst-case over the set of analytic specifications)
mapping.b_element: characterizing the sharp lower bound of the identified set (worst-case over the set of preference distributions consistent with the data)

brief_justification: The only overlap is a shallow worst-case-over-a-set heuristic: paper A is a normative/rhetorical critique proposing conventions (judge a multiverse by its worst universe, treat size as weakness) with no formal governing process, whereas paper B is a formal partial-identification result deriving sharp bounds on a causal estimand under endogenous menu assignment with heterogeneous preferences; there is no role-for-role correspondence of driving machinery, so they do not share an underlying method.
